name: CHEMISTRY (Theory)
question_paper_html:
Compartment Exam - 2022

Code: 56/6/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 12 questions.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:35

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 12 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into three Sections A, B and C.
  3. Section A - Questions no. 1 to 3 are very short answer type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  4. Section B - Questions no. 4 to 11 are short answer type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  5. Section C - Question no. 12 is case based question, carrying 5 marks.
  6. Use of log tables and calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A
1.

Answer the following questions (Any two): 2{tex}\times{/tex}1=2

(i)

Why is alternating current used for measuring resistance of an electrolytic solution?
(ii)

State Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions.
(iii)

Depict the Galvanic cell in which the following reaction takes place:
Mg (s) + Cu2+ (aq) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Mg2+ (aq) + Cu (s)
2.
  1. For a reaction R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} P, half life (t1/2) is independent of the initial concentration of reactants. What is the order of the reaction?
  2. Write one difference between the order and molecularity of a reaction.
 2
3.
  1. Arrange the following compounds in the increasing order of their acidic strength:
    {tex}Cl - CH_2 - COOH, Cl - \underset {\underset {Cl}|}CH -COOH, CCl_3 - COOH{/tex}
  2. Write the IUPAC name of the given compound:
    CH3COCH2COCH3
 2

SECTION B
4.(i)

The variation in the concentration (R) vs. time (t) plot is given below. Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure:

  1. Predict the order of the reaction.
  2. What is the slope of the curve indicate?
  3. What are the units of the rate constant k?
 3

OR
(ii)

A first order reaction takes 24 minutes for 50% decomposition. Calculate the time required for 25% decomposition. (Given: log 4 = 0.6021, log 3 = 0.4771)

 3
5.(i)
  1. Which is the most common oxidation state in Lanthanoids?
  2. Why is there a gradual decrease in the atomic sizes of transition metals in a series with increasing atomic numbers?
  3. Calculate the number of unpaired electrons in the following gaseous ions:
    V3+, Ti3+
 3

OR
(ii)
  1. The electronic configuration of Ce is:
    58Ce = [Xe]54 4f1 5d1 6s2
    Calculate the spin only magnetic moment of Ce3+ ion.
  2. Why is copper regarded as a transition element although copper atom has completely filled d-orbitals in its ground state?
  3. Why is Sc3+ colourless in aqueous solution whereas Ti3+ is coloured?
 3
6.

Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure:

  1. Define the process depicted in the above figure.
  2. Can this process be used in the coagulation of the lyophobic sols?
  3. What is coagulation?
 3

OR

Define the following terms:

  1. Adsorption
  2. Lyophobic sol
  3. Multimolecular colloid
 3
7.

The conductivity of 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 M methanoic acid is 5.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 S cm-1 and its {tex}\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}^0{/tex} has a value 400 S cm2 mol-1. Calculate its molar conductivity and degree of dissociation.

 3
8.(i)

Choose the primary amine from the following compounds:
(C2H5)2CHNH2, (C2H5)2NH, (CH3)3N

 1
(ii)

Complete the following:
{tex}\text{CH}_3\text{Br} \xrightarrow{\text {KCN}} \text A \xrightarrow{\text {LiAlH}_4} \text B{/tex}

 2
9.
  1. Write the hybridization, shape and magnetic character of [CoF6]3-.
    [Atomic number of Co = 27]
  2. Write the formula of Pentaamminechloridocobalt(III)chloride.
 3
10.

Account for the following: (3{tex}\times{/tex}1=3)

(i)

Why do transition elements show variable oxidation states?
(ii)

{tex}E^o_{Cu^{2+}/Cu}{/tex} value for copper is highly positive.
(iii)

Cr2+ is a strong reducing agent.

 OR

What is Lanthanoid contraction? Write two consequences of Lanthanoid contraction.

 3
11.
  1. Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis. Why?
  2. Aniline is a weaker base than alkyl amines. Why?
  3. Arrange the following in increasing order of basic strength in aqueous solution:
    (CH3CH2)2NH, (C2H5)3N, C2H5NH2
 3

SECTION C
12.

Read the following passage and answer the questions that follow: (1+1+1+2=5)

A class of organic molecules which contain a carbon atom connected to an oxygen atom by a double bond is called Aldehydes and Ketones. It is called as carbonyl group. Aldehydes are prepared by the oxidation of alcohols. Formaldehyde is sold in an aqueous solution called formalin. Propanone, a simplest ketone is commercially prepared by fermenting corn or by oxidation of propan-2-ol. Carboxylic acids also have carbonyl carbon. They can be prepared by the oxidation of alcohols and aldehydes.
Formic acid was first isolated by the distillation of red ants. It is partially responsible for the pain and irritation of ant and wasp stings. Aldehydes undergo many nucleophilic addition reactions. They can be reduced to primary alcohols. The aldehydes with -hydrogen undergo aldol condensation and the aldehydes without -hydrogen undergo Cannizzaro reaction.
Ketones are highly reactive, although less so than aldehydes. Ketones are obtained by oxidation of secondary alcohols. Ketones possessing {tex}\alpha{/tex}-hydrogens also undergo aldol condensation. Carboxylic acids occur widely in nature and are used in the production of plastics, esters, etc. Aspirin is prepared from acetic acid. Similar to aldehydes and ketones, carboxylic acids can be halogenated at the {tex}\alpha{/tex}-carbon by reacting with a halogen in presence of phosphorus.
(i)

Which of the following compounds would undergo aldol condensation?
Methanal, Benzaldehyde, Ethanal
(ii)

Write the chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone.
(iii)

Write the reagent required in the following reaction:
CH2 = CH - CH2OH {tex}\xrightarrow {?}{/tex} CH2 = CH - CHO
(iv)(a)

An alcohol 'A', (C3H8O) on oxidation gives compound 'B'. 'B' gives negative Tollens' test and reacts with hydrazine to give compound 'C'. 'B' reacts withNaOHandI2$ to give yellow precipitate of 'D'. Identify 'A', 'B', 'C' and 'D'.

OR
(b)

Write the chemical reactions for Clemmensen reduction.

answer_key_html:
Compartment Exam - 2022

Code: 56/6/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 12 questions.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:35

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 12 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into three Sections A, B and C.
  3. Section A - Questions no. 1 to 3 are very short answer type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  4. Section B - Questions no. 4 to 11 are short answer type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  5. Section C - Question no. 12 is case based question, carrying 5 marks.
  6. Use of log tables and calculators is not allowed.

SECTION : A
1.

Answer the following questions (Any two): 2{tex}\times{/tex}1=2

2
(i)

Why is alternating current used for measuring resistance of an electrolytic solution?

Solution:

The alternating current is used to prevent electrolysis so that the concentration of ions in the solution remains constant.

 1
(ii)

State Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions.

Solution:

Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions. The law states that limiting molar conductivity of an electrolyte can be represented as the sum of the individual contributions of the anion and cation of the electrolyte.

 1
(iii)

Depict the Galvanic cell in which the following reaction takes place:
Mg (s) + Cu2+ (aq) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Mg2+ (aq) + Cu (s)

Solution:

Mg(s) | Mg2+ (aq) || Cu2+ (aq) | Cu(s)

 1
2.
  1. For a reaction R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} P, half life (t1/2) is independent of the initial concentration of reactants. What is the order of the reaction?
  2. Write one difference between the order and molecularity of a reaction.
Solution:
  1. For a reaction R → B half-life (t1/2) is observed to be independent of the initial concentration of reactants. This is the first order reaction.
  2. Order Molecularity
    It is defined as the sum of powers of concentration terms in a rate equation. It is defined as no. of molecules or atoms or ions participating in a rate determining step
    It is an experimental quantity. It is a theoretical concept.
2
3.
  1. Arrange the following compounds in the increasing order of their acidic strength:
    {tex}Cl - CH_2 - COOH, Cl - \underset {\underset {Cl}|}CH -COOH, CCl_3 - COOH{/tex}
  2. Write the IUPAC name of the given compound:
    CH3COCH2COCH3
Solution:
  1. Greater the number of electron withdrawing substituents, greater would be the dispersal of the negative charge and hence stronger will be the acid.
    {tex}Cl - CH_2 - COOH < Cl - \underset {\underset {Cl}|}CH -COOH < CCl_3 - COOH{/tex}​​
  2. The IUPAC name of the compound is: Pentan-2,4-dione
 2

SECTION : B
4.(i)

The variation in the concentration (R) vs. time (t) plot is given below. Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure:

  1. Predict the order of the reaction.
  2. What is the slope of the curve indicate?
  3. What are the units of the rate constant k?
Solution:
  1. First order reaction
  2. Rate constat (k) of reaction
  3. The unit of rate constant is time-1
 3

OR
(ii)

A first order reaction takes 24 minutes for 50% decomposition. Calculate the time required for 25% decomposition. (Given: log 4 = 0.6021, log 3 = 0.4771)

Solution:

For a first order reaction
t = {tex}\frac{2.303}{k}{/tex}log {tex}\frac{[R]_0}{R}{/tex}
k = {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min } {/tex}log {tex}\frac{100}{100-25}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min } \frac{\log 4}{3}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min }{/tex}(log 4 - log 3)
= {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min }{/tex}(0.6021 - 0.4771)
= 1.44 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-2 min-1
The time when 75% of the reaction completed can be calculated as
t = {tex}\frac{2.303}{k}{/tex}log {tex}\frac{100}{100-75}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2.303}{1.44 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex} log4
= {tex}\frac{2.303}{1.44 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex}(0.06021)
= 96.3 min (approximately)

 3
5.(i)
  1. Which is the most common oxidation state in Lanthanoids?
  2. Why is there a gradual decrease in the atomic sizes of transition metals in a series with increasing atomic numbers?
  3. Calculate the number of unpaired electrons in the following gaseous ions:
    V3+, Ti3+
Solution:
  1. +3 is the most common oxidation state shown by lanthanides
  2. There is a gradual decrease in the atomic sizes of transition elements in a series with increasing atomic numbers due to poor shielding effect of d-electrons, the net electrostatic attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electrons.
  3. Vi3+ no of unpaired electron is 2
    Ti3+ no of unpaired electron is 1
 3

OR
(ii)
  1. The electronic configuration of Ce is:
    58Ce = [Xe]54 4f1 5d1 6s2
    Calculate the spin only magnetic moment of Ce3+ ion.
  2. Why is copper regarded as a transition element although copper atom has completely filled d-orbitals in its ground state?
  3. Why is Sc3+ colourless in aqueous solution whereas Ti3+ is coloured?
Solution:
  1. Number of unpaired electrons = n = 1 {tex}\mu{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{n(n+2)} = \sqrt 8 = 2\sqrt2{/tex} = 2.828 BM
  2. Because it has incompletely filled d orbitals in one of its oxidation state (Cu2+).
  3. Sc3+ ion has completely empty 3d-orbital, i.e., no unpaired electrons are present. As d-d transitions are not possible, Sc3+ ion is colourless. Ti3+ ion has one unpaired electron in 3d-orbital. As d-d transitions are possible, Ti3+ ion is coloured.
 3
6.

Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure:

  1. Define the process depicted in the above figure.
  2. Can this process be used in the coagulation of the lyophobic sols?
  3. What is coagulation?
Solution:
  1. The movement of colloidal particles under an applied electric potential.
  2. Yes this process be used in the coagulation of the lyophobic sols.
  3. The process of settling colloidal particles is coagulation.
 3

OR

Define the following terms:

  1. Adsorption
  2. Lyophobic sol
  3. Multimolecular colloid
Solution:

Chapter Delete from the Textbook.

 3
7.

The conductivity of 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 M methanoic acid is 5.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-5 S cm-1 and its {tex}\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}^0{/tex} has a value 400 S cm2 mol-1. Calculate its molar conductivity and degree of dissociation.

Solution:

{tex}\Lambda_m{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1000 \times \mathrm{K}}{\mathrm{M}}{/tex} S cm2 mol-1
{tex} \Lambda_m{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1000 \times 5.25 \times 10^{-5}}{2.5 \times 10^{-4}}{/tex} - S cm2 mol-1
= 210 S cm2mol-1
{tex} \wedge_m^0 \mathrm{HCOOH}{/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\circ} \mathrm{HCOO}^{-}+\lambda^{\circ} \mathrm{H}^{+}{/tex}
= (50.5 + 349.5) S cm2mol-1
= 400 S cm2mol-1
{tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\Lambda_m }{\Lambda_m^{\circ}}{/tex}
{tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\frac{210}{400}{/tex}
= 0.525

 3

Answer the following questions:

3
8.(i)

Choose the primary amine from the following compounds:
(C2H5)2CHNH2, (C2H5)2NH, (CH3)3N

Solution:

The primary amine among the given compounds is (C2H5)2CHNH2.
In this compound, (C2H5)2CHNH2, you can see that the nitrogen atom (N) is bonded to two hydrogen atoms (H) and one carbon atom (C), which is the defining characteristic of a primary amine. Primary amines have the general structure R-NH2, where R represents an alkyl or aryl group. In this case, (C2H5)2CH represents the alkyl group, and NH2 represents the amino group bonded to the primary carbon.

 1
(ii)

Complete the following:
{tex}\text{CH}_3\text{Br} \xrightarrow{\text {KCN}} \text A \xrightarrow{\text {LiAlH}_4} \text B{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3\mathrm{Br}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{KCN}}{\mathrm{CH}}_3\mathrm{CN}\xrightarrow{{\mathrm{LiAlH}}_4}{\mathrm{CH}}_3{\mathrm{CH}}_2{\mathrm{NH}}_2{/tex}

 2
9.
  1. Write the hybridization, shape and magnetic character of [CoF6]3-.
    [Atomic number of Co = 27]
  2. Write the formula of Pentaamminechloridocobalt(III)chloride.
Solution:
  1. The central Co atom is sp3d2 hybridised. It forms octahedral complex with paramagnetic behavior due to presence of 4 unpaired electrons.
  2. [Co(NH3)5Cl]Cl2
 3
10.

Account for the following: (3{tex}\times{/tex}1=3)

3
(i)

Why do transition elements show variable oxidation states?

Solution:

ns and (n-1) d electrons of transition metal have very little difference in the energies and hence both can participate in bonding, which results in variable oxidation states. When ns electrons take part in bonding, they exhibit lower oxidation states whereas when (n -1 )d electrons along with ns electrons participate in bonding, they exhibit higher oxidation states.

 1
(ii)

{tex}E^o_{Cu^{2+}/Cu}{/tex} value for copper is highly positive.

Solution:

Copper has a high atomization energy ΔaHΘ and Low hydration energy ΔhydHΘ. Due to which Eo value is positive.

 1
(iii)

Cr2+ is a strong reducing agent.

Solution:

Cr2+ is a strong reducing agent because after the loss of one electron Cr2+ becomes Cr3+ which has more stable t2g3 (half-filled) configuration.

 1

 OR

What is Lanthanoid contraction? Write two consequences of Lanthanoid contraction.

Solution:

The steady decrease in the size of lanthanoid ions (M3+) with the increase in atomic number is called lanthanoid contraction.
Consequences of lanthanoid contraction:

  1. Separation of lanthanoids is difficult due to lanthanoid contraction because due to this contraction their ionic radii is very small.
  2. The basic strength of hydrides decreases from La(OH)3 to Lu(OH)3. Due to lanthanoid contraction, the size of M3+ ions decreases and there is increase in the covalent character in M - OH bond.
 3
11.
  1. Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis. Why?
  2. Aniline is a weaker base than alkyl amines. Why?
  3. Arrange the following in increasing order of basic strength in aqueous solution:
    (CH3CH2)2NH, (C2H5)3N, C2H5NH2
Solution:
  1. Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel phthalimide synthesis as aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution with the salt formed by phthalimide.
  2. Lone pair on Nitrogen in aniline are involved in resonance with the benzene ring and therefore not available for donation to that extent as in NH3 According to Lewis theory, the species which easily loses a pair of electron is Lewis base, Since, ammonia can lose easily electron pair while aniline cannot, thus ammonia is a stronger base than aniline.
  3. +I effect of alkyl group increases electron density on −N of amine and increases basic strength. Thus (C2H5)2NH is strongest base. In aniline, the lone pair of electrons of N is in delocalization with ring which makes it less available and least basic. Two Methyl substitutions increases basic strength due to its inductive effect but the steric effect also influences it.
 3

SECTION : C
12.

Read the following passage and answer the questions that follow: (1+1+1+2=5)
A class of organic molecules which contain a carbon atom connected to an oxygen atom by a double bond is called Aldehydes and Ketones. It is called as carbonyl group. Aldehydes are prepared by the oxidation of alcohols. Formaldehyde is sold in an aqueous solution called formalin. Propanone, a simplest ketone is commercially prepared by fermenting corn or by oxidation of propan-2-ol. Carboxylic acids also have carbonyl carbon. They can be prepared by the oxidation of alcohols and aldehydes.
Formic acid was first isolated by the distillation of red ants. It is partially responsible for the pain and irritation of ant and wasp stings. Aldehydes undergo many nucleophilic addition reactions. They can be reduced to primary alcohols. The aldehydes with -hydrogen undergo aldol condensation and the aldehydes without -hydrogen undergo Cannizzaro reaction.
Ketones are highly reactive, although less so than aldehydes. Ketones are obtained by oxidation of secondary alcohols. Ketones possessing α-hydrogens also undergo aldol condensation. Carboxylic acids occur widely in nature and are used in the production of plastics, esters, etc. Aspirin is prepared from acetic acid. Similar to aldehydes and ketones, carboxylic acids can be halogenated at the α-carbon by reacting with a halogen in presence of phosphorus.

  1. Which of the following compounds would undergo aldol condensation? (1)
    Methanal, Benzaldehyde, Ethanal
  2. Write the chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone. (1)
  3. Write the reagent required in the following reaction: (1) 
    CH2 = CH - CH2OH {tex}\xrightarrow {?}{/tex} CH2 = CH - CHO
    1. An alcohol 'A', (C3H8O) on oxidation gives compound 'B'. 'B' gives negative Tollens' test and reacts with hydrazine to give compound 'C'. 'B' reacts withNaOHandI2$ to give yellow precipitate of 'D'. Identify 'A', 'B', 'C' and 'D'. (2)

      OR

    2. Write the chemical reactions for the following: (2)
      1. Clemmensen reduction
      2. HVZ reaction
Solution:
  1. Benzaldehyde undergoes aldol condensation in an alkaline medium.
  2. The reaction with sodium hypoiodite is the distinguishing test between propanal and propanone. This test is known as the iodoform test.
  3. Pyridinium chlorochromate (C5H5NHCrO3Cl) or Cu at 573 K are the reagents required.
    1. {tex}(\mathrm X)-{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{OH}{\underset\vert{\mathrm{CH}}}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3\xrightarrow{{\mathrm K}_2{\mathrm{Cr}}_2{\mathrm O}_7}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\overset O{\overset{\vert\vert}{\mathrm C}}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3(\mathrm y){/tex}
      (X) with molecular formula C3H8O is an alcohol. CH3-CH2-CH2-OH, propanol.
      But since K2Cr2O7 being a strong oxidising agent would oxidise 1o alcohol to carboxylic acid, which will give negative iodoform test, thus (X) has to be a 2o alcohol.
      Acetone would give positive iodoform test.

      OR

    2. (I) 
      (II) 
 5
answer_key_text: Compartment Exam - 2022 Code: 56/6/1 (set-1) Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate. Please check that this question paper contains 12 questions. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period. CHEMISTRY (Theory) Time allowed:2 hours Maximum Marks:35 General Instructions : Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them: This question paper contains 12 questions. All questions are compulsory. This question paper is divided into three Sections A, B and C . Section A - Questions no. 1 to 3 are very short answer type questions, carrying 2 marks each. Section B - Questions no. 4 to 11 are short answer type questions, carrying 3 marks each. Section C - Question no. 12 is case based question, carrying 5 marks. Use of log tables and calculators is not allowed. SECTION : A 1. Answer the following questions (Any two ): 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1=2 2 (i) Why is alternating current used for measuring resistance of an electrolytic solution? Solution: The alternating current is used to prevent electrolysis so that the concentration of ions in the solution remains constant. 1 (ii) State Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions. Solution: Kohlrausch's law of independent migration of ions. The law states that limiting molar conductivity of an electrolyte can be represented as the sum of the individual contributions of the anion and cation of the electrolyte. 1 (iii) Depict the Galvanic cell in which the following reaction takes place: Mg (s) + Cu 2+ (aq) {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} Mg 2+ (aq) + Cu (s) Solution: Mg(s) | Mg 2+ (aq) || Cu 2+ (aq) | Cu(s) 1 2. For a reaction R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} P, half life (t 1/2 ) is independent of the initial concentration of reactants. What is the order of the reaction? Write one difference between the order and molecularity of a reaction. Solution: For a reaction R → B half-life (t1/2) is observed to be independent of the initial concentration of reactants. This is the first order reaction. Order Molecularity It is defined as the sum of powers of concentration terms in a rate equation. It is defined as no. of molecules or atoms or ions participating in a rate determining step It is an experimental quantity. It is a theoretical concept. 2 3. Arrange the following compounds in the increasing order of their acidic strength: {tex}Cl - CH_2 - COOH, Cl - \underset {\underset {Cl}|}CH -COOH, CCl_3 - COOH{/tex} Write the IUPAC name of the given compound: CH 3 COCH 2 COCH 3 Solution: Greater the number of electron withdrawing substituents, greater would be the dispersal of the negative charge and hence stronger will be the acid. {tex}Cl - CH_2 - COOH < Cl - \underset {\underset {Cl}|}CH -COOH < CCl_3 - COOH{/tex} ​​ The IUPAC name of the compound is: Pentan-2,4-dione 2 SECTION : B 4. (i) The variation in the concentration (R) vs. time (t) plot is given below. Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure: Predict the order of the reaction. What is the slope of the curve indicate? What are the units of the rate constant k? Solution: First order reaction Rate constat (k) of reaction The unit of rate constant is time -1 3 OR (ii) A first order reaction takes 24 minutes for 50% decomposition. Calculate the time required for 25% decomposition. (Given: log 4 = 0.6021, log 3 = 0.4771) Solution: For a first order reaction t = {tex}\frac{2.303}{k}{/tex} log {tex}\frac{[R]_0}{R}{/tex} k = {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min } {/tex} log {tex}\frac{100}{100-25}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min } \frac{\log 4}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min }{/tex} (log 4 - log 3) = {tex}\frac{2.303}{20 \min }{/tex} (0.6021 - 0.4771) = 1.44 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -2 min -1 The time when 75% of the reaction completed can be calculated as t = {tex}\frac{2.303}{k}{/tex} log {tex}\frac{100}{100-75}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2.303}{1.44 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex} log4 = {tex}\frac{2.303}{1.44 \times 10^{-2}}{/tex} (0.06021) = 96.3 min (approximately) 3 5. (i) Which is the most common oxidation state in Lanthanoids? Why is there a gradual decrease in the atomic sizes of transition metals in a series with increasing atomic numbers? Calculate the number of unpaired electrons in the following gaseous ions: V 3+ , Ti 3+ Solution: +3 is the most common oxidation state shown by lanthanides There is a gradual decrease in the atomic sizes of transition elements in a series with increasing atomic numbers due to poor shielding effect of d-electrons, the net electrostatic attraction between the nucleus and the outermost electrons. Vi 3+ no of unpaired electron is 2 Ti 3+ no of unpaired electron is 1 3 OR (ii) The electronic configuration of Ce is: 58 Ce = [Xe] 54 4f 1 5d 1 6s 2 Calculate the spin only magnetic moment of Ce 3+ ion. Why is copper regarded as a transition element although copper atom has completely filled d-orbitals in its ground state? Why is Sc 3+ colourless in aqueous solution whereas Ti 3+ is coloured? Solution: Number of unpaired electrons = n = 1 {tex}\mu{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{n(n+2)} = \sqrt 8 = 2\sqrt2{/tex} = 2.828 BM Because it has incompletely filled d orbitals in one of its oxidation state (Cu 2+ ). Sc 3+ ion has completely empty 3d-orbital, i.e., no unpaired electrons are present. As d-d transitions are not possible, Sc 3+ ion is colourless. Ti 3+ ion has one unpaired electron in 3d-orbital. As d-d transitions are possible, Ti 3+ ion is coloured. 3 6. Answer the following questions on the basis of the given figure: Define the process depicted in the above figure. Can this process be used in the coagulation of the lyophobic sols? What is coagulation? Solution: The movement of colloidal particles under an applied electric potential. Yes this process be used in the coagulation of the lyophobic sols. The process of settling colloidal particles is coagulation. 3 OR Define the following terms: Adsorption Lyophobic sol Multimolecular colloid Solution: Chapter Delete from the Textbook. 3 7. The conductivity of 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -4 M methanoic acid is 5.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -5 S cm -1 and its {tex}\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}^0{/tex} has a value 400 S cm 2 mol -1 . Calculate its molar conductivity and degree of dissociation. Solution: {tex}\Lambda_m{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1000 \times \mathrm{K}}{\mathrm{M}}{/tex} S cm 2 mol -1 {tex} \Lambda_m{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1000 \times 5.25 \times 10^{-5}}{2.5 \times 10^{-4}}{/tex} - S cm 2 mol -1 = 210 S cm 2 mol -1 {tex} \wedge_m^0 \mathrm{HCOOH}{/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\circ} \mathrm{HCOO}^{-}+\lambda^{\circ} \mathrm{H}^{+}{/tex} = (50.5 + 349.5) S cm 2 mol -1 = 400 S cm 2 mol -1 {tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\Lambda_m }{\Lambda_m^{\circ}}{/tex} {tex}\alpha{/tex} = {tex}\frac{210}{400}{/tex} = 0.525 3 Answer the following questions: 3 8. (i) Choose the primary amine from the following compounds: (C 2 H 5 ) 2 CHNH 2 , (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH, (CH 3 ) 3 N Solution: The primary amine among the given compounds is (C 2 H5) 2 CHNH 2 . In this compound, (C 2 H 5 ) 2 CHNH 2 , you can see that the nitrogen atom (N) is bonded to two hydrogen atoms (H) and one carbon atom (C), which is the defining characteristic of a primary amine. Primary amines have the general structure R-NH 2 , where R represents an alkyl or aryl group. In this case, (C 2 H 5 ) 2 CH represents the alkyl group, and NH 2 represents the amino group bonded to the primary carbon. 1 (ii) Complete the following: {tex}\text{CH}_3\text{Br} \xrightarrow{\text {KCN}} \text A \xrightarrow{\text {LiAlH}_4} \text B{/tex} Solution: {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3\mathrm{Br}\xrightarrow{\mathrm{KCN}}{\mathrm{CH}}_3\mathrm{CN}\xrightarrow{{\mathrm{LiAlH}}_4}{\mathrm{CH}}_3{\mathrm{CH}}_2{\mathrm{NH}}_2{/tex} 2 9. Write the hybridization, shape and magnetic character of [CoF 6 ] 3- . [Atomic number of Co = 27] Write the formula of Pentaamminechloridocobalt(III)chloride. Solution: The central Co atom is sp 3 d 2 hybridised. It forms octahedral complex with paramagnetic behavior due to presence of 4 unpaired electrons. [Co(NH 3 ) 5 Cl]Cl 2 3 10. Account for the following: (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 1=3) 3 (i) Why do transition elements show variable oxidation states? Solution: ns and (n-1) d electrons of transition metal have very little difference in the energies and hence both can participate in bonding, which results in variable oxidation states. When ns electrons take part in bonding, they exhibit lower oxidation states whereas when (n -1 )d electrons along with ns electrons participate in bonding, they exhibit higher oxidation states. 1 (ii) {tex}E^o_{Cu^{2+}/Cu}{/tex} value for copper is highly positive. Solution: Copper has a high atomization energy Δ a HΘ and Low hydration energy Δ hyd HΘ. Due to which E o value is positive. 1 (iii) Cr 2+ is a strong reducing agent. Solution: Cr 2+ is a strong reducing agent because after the loss of one electron Cr 2+ becomes Cr 3+ which has more stable t 2 g 3 (half-filled) configuration. 1 OR What is Lanthanoid contraction? Write two consequences of Lanthanoid contraction. Solution: The steady decrease in the size of lanthanoid ions (M 3+ ) with the increase in atomic number is called lanthanoid contraction. Consequences of lanthanoid contraction: Separation of lanthanoids is difficult due to lanthanoid contraction because due to this contraction their ionic radii is very small. The basic strength of hydrides decreases from La(OH) 3 to Lu(OH) 3 . Due to lanthanoid contraction, the size of M 3+ ions decreases and there is increase in the covalent character in M - OH bond. 3 11. Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis. Why? Aniline is a weaker base than alkyl amines. Why? Arrange the following in increasing order of basic strength in aqueous solution: (CH 3 CH 2 ) 2 NH, (C 2 H 5 ) 3 N, C 2 H 5 NH 2 Solution: Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel phthalimide synthesis as aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution with the salt formed by phthalimide. Lone pair on Nitrogen in aniline are involved in resonance with the benzene ring and therefore not available for donation to that extent as in NH 3 According to Lewis theory, the species which easily loses a pair of electron is Lewis base, Since, ammonia can lose easily electron pair while aniline cannot, thus ammonia is a stronger base than aniline. +I effect of alkyl group increases electron density on −N of amine and increases basic strength. Thus (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH is strongest base. In aniline, the lone pair of electrons of N is in delocalization with ring which makes it less available and least basic. Two Methyl substitutions increases basic strength due to its inductive effect but the steric effect also influences it. 3 SECTION : C 12. Read the following passage and answer the questions that follow: (1+1+1+2=5) A class of organic molecules which contain a carbon atom connected to an oxygen atom by a double bond is called Aldehydes and Ketones. It is called as carbonyl group. Aldehydes are prepared by the oxidation of alcohols. Formaldehyde is sold in an aqueous solution called formalin. Propanone, a simplest ketone is commercially prepared by fermenting corn or by oxidation of propan-2-ol. Carboxylic acids also have carbonyl carbon. They can be prepared by the oxidation of alcohols and aldehydes. Formic acid was first isolated by the distillation of red ants. It is partially responsible for the pain and irritation of ant and wasp stings. Aldehydes undergo many nucleophilic addition reactions. They can be reduced to primary alcohols. The aldehydes with -hydrogen undergo aldol condensation and the aldehydes without -hydrogen undergo Cannizzaro reaction. Ketones are highly reactive, although less so than aldehydes. Ketones are obtained by oxidation of secondary alcohols. Ketones possessing α-hydrogens also undergo aldol condensation. Carboxylic acids occur widely in nature and are used in the production of plastics, esters, etc. Aspirin is prepared from acetic acid. Similar to aldehydes and ketones, carboxylic acids can be halogenated at the α-carbon by reacting with a halogen in presence of phosphorus. Which of the following compounds would undergo aldol condensation? (1) Methanal, Benzaldehyde, Ethanal Write the chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone. (1) Write the reagent required in the following reaction: (1) CH 2 = CH - CH 2 OH {tex}\xrightarrow {?}{/tex} CH 2 = CH - CHO An alcohol 'A', (C 3 H 8 O) on oxidation gives compound 'B'. 'B' gives negative Tollens' test and reacts with hydrazine to give compound 'C'. 'B' reacts withNaOHandI2$ to give yellow precipitate of 'D'. Identify 'A', 'B', 'C' and 'D'. (2) OR Write the chemical reactions for the following: (2) Clemmensen reduction HVZ reaction Solution: Benzaldehyde undergoes aldol condensation in an alkaline medium. The reaction with sodium hypoiodite is the distinguishing test between propanal and propanone. This test is known as the iodoform test. Pyridinium chlorochromate (C 5 H 5 NHCrO 3 Cl) or Cu at 573 K are the reagents required. {tex}(\mathrm X)-{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\underset{OH}{\underset\vert{\mathrm{CH}}}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3\xrightarrow{{\mathrm K}_2{\mathrm{Cr}}_2{\mathrm O}_7}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\overset O{\overset{\vert\vert}{\mathrm C}}-{\mathrm{CH}}_3(\mathrm y){/tex} (X) with molecular formula C 3 H 8 O is an alcohol. CH 3 -CH 2- CH 2 -OH, propanol. But since K 2 Cr 2 O 7 being a strong oxidising agent would oxidise 1 o alcohol to carboxylic acid, which will give negative iodoform test, thus (X) has to be a 2 o alcohol. Acetone would give positive iodoform test. OR (I) (II) 5
